title: Control Your Speed
message: Speaking too fast makes you sound nervous. Aim for 130-150 words per minute (WPM).
exercise: Take a short news article. Set a 1-minute timer and try to read exactly 130 words, placing intentional 1-second pauses after every period.

title: Slay the Fillers
message: Fillers like 'um', 'uh', and 'like' degrade your authority. Replace them with quiet, confident pauses.
exercise: Speak about your favourite movie for 1 minute. Each time you feel the urge to say 'like' or 'um', close your mouth, swallow, and pause for 1 second instead.

title: Use Pitch and Modulation
message: A flat monotone voice bores listeners. Slide your pitch up for excitement and drop it down for key emphasis.
exercise: Perform the 'Siren Exercise': make a 'whoop' sound sliding your voice from your lowest comfortable register to your highest, then back down.

title: Echo and Validate
message: Good conversation is 60% listening. Restate what the other person said to show alignment before adding your perspective.
exercise: In your next conversation, start your responses with 'So, if I understand correctly, you're saying...' to validate the other person's point.

title: Start with a Hook
message: Begin stories with action or an intriguing question instead of dry background facts to grab attention immediately.
exercise: Create a 30-second introduction for a story that starts with: 'I will never forget the day when...' or 'Have you ever had to choose between...'

title: The STAR Method
message: Structure behavioral questions with: Situation, Task, Action, and Result to deliver concise and impact-driven answers.
exercise: Write down a past achievement. Script a 2-minute answer allocating 15s to Situation/Task, 45s to Action, and 15s to the quantitative Result.

title: Gesture within the Box
message: Keep hand gestures natural and centered between your chest and waist. Avoid wild hand-waving or stiff arm postures.
exercise: Stand in front of a mirror (or look at your camera feed) and speak for 1 minute. Keep your elbows relaxed near your sides and gesturate intentionally only to highlight action verbs.
